meta.title: Sapporo Teine Ski Resort
meta.description: Snow & Culture resort package linking powder, a 2M city, Jozankei onsen, and Otaru.

hero.title:
Sapporo Teine
Ski Resort
hero.tagline: Powder by day, sushi and onsen by night—an urban-adjacent snow & culture resort.

live.label: Today
live.subtitle: World-class snow, 40 min from the city

honoka.label: Honoka onsen
honoka.note: Transit hub on the way down

corridor.title:
Teine, Kokusai, Jozankei,
Otaru—one package.
corridor.lead: World-class powder, metro infrastructure, a historic port, and hot-spring culture within 30–40 km—a rare urban-adjacent, distributed resort unlike self-contained mega-resorts.
corridor.noticeBody: Elite powder by day, Masazushi or UNWIND's bar by night, Hoheikyo's rotenburo—a stay package with contrasts rarely found elsewhere.
corridor.imgAlt: Snow in west Sapporo—a corridor linking city, mountains, and sea

primary.title: Olympic powder × city access
primary.lead: About 40 min from downtown Sapporo. 15 courses including the 5.4 km Rainbow run on 1972 Olympic slopes—then GARAKU soup curry or beer-garden Genghis Khan.
primary.imgAlt: Powder at Teine—mountain snow minutes from the city

secondary.title: 24h recovery at Honoka
secondary.lead: Teine Onsen Honoka at the base—super-sento culture with rock baths and dining, an affordable transit stop for inbound FIT guests.

pass.title:
Snow, city, and tradition—
seamlessly linked.
pass.lead: For high-value FIT guests—private bath at Koganeyu after skiing, Masazushi in Otaru at night. Chalet Ivy Jozankei offers in-room rotenburo across every suite.
pass.imgAlt: Jozankei and Otaru linked—a network of base camps

recovery.title:
After skiing,
fill up on onsen and food.
recovery.lead: Hoheikyo's snow-view rotenburo with Indian curry, Honoka at Teine, O-Spa at Otaru IC—layered fatigue recovery after winter sports.
recovery.whisper: Private baths at Koganeyu welcome tattoos (from ¥3,800 / 90 min)—a strong hook for privacy-focused Western guests.

guidesTeasers.title: Stays · onsen · dining — full lists inside
guidesTeasers.lead: Corridor venues with phones, official links, and maps on dedicated pages.

stay.label: Stays
stay.title: 5 curated stays
stay.hook: From Jozankei wellness to Otaru heritage hotels.

food.label: Dining
food.title: 6 curated restaurants
food.hook: Triangle Market, Masazushi, GARAKU—culture hooks that drive circulation.

journey.title:
Three days,
self-guided corridor.
journey.lead: A model course for independent inbound travelers—snow & wellness resort package.

01.title: Morning: Triangle Market
01.body: Seafood bowl at Takeda (from 7:00)—ultimate pre-ski breakfast

02.title: Day: Sapporo Teine
02.body: Powder on Olympic slopes, or head to Sapporo Kokusai

03.title: Evening: Honoka · Koganeyu
03.body: 24h super-sento or private sulfur spring recovery

04.title: Night: Otaru
04.body: Masazushi, Otaru Bine, UNWIND bar nightlife

05.title: Stay: Jozankei or Otaru
05.body: Chalet Ivy rotenburo, OMO5 neighborhood map exploration

corridor.title: Complete corridor guide
corridor.body: The 30–40 km loop of Teine, Kokusai, Jozankei, and Otaru—MaaS needs, multilingual storytelling, and the shift to luxury wellness.

authentic.title: Authenticity assets
authentic.body: UNWIND in a former hotel, red-brick beer garden, Triangle Market chaos, Hoheikyo curry—moving from surface consumption to deep cultural experience.

luxury.title: 5 luxury wellness stays
luxury.body: Chalet Ivy (every suite with rotenburo, 16 EV chargers), Kuriyasuizan (gastronomy ryokan), Morino Uta (forest spa), UNWIND (heritage), OMO5 (universal design).

access.note: ~40 min from downtown Sapporo · ~80 min from New Chitose · Bus from JR Teine · Parking available
access.places: 北海道Hokkaido札幌市Sapporo手稲区Teine-ku手稲本町Teinehoncho

hero.title:
Local gastronomy,
six faces.
hero.tagline: From Otaru seafood to Sapporo-born soup curry and pioneer-era Genghis Khan

nearbyFood.notice: Hours, reservations, and dietary accommodations—check each venue's official site.
nearbyFood.introLead: Terroir-rooted dining is the strongest motivator for travel in this corridor.
nearbyFood.introBody: Six inbound-ready picks—multilingual service, diversity, accessibility, or experience that exceeds those baselines.
nearbyFood.mapLead: Layer food, onsen, and hubs across Teine, Jozankei, Otaru, and central Sapporo.

nearbyFood.spotsTitle: 6 curated dining spots for inbound guests
nearbyFood.spotsLead: Ordered for easy pairing before/after skiing and corridor moves.

masazushi.title: Masazushi Honten
masazushi.lead: Otaru sushi street icon—vegetarian and assistance-dog ready.

takeda.title: Takeda Market Diner
takeda.lead: Inside Triangle Market—ultimate pre-ski morning fuel.

otaru-bine.title: Otaru Bine
otaru-bine.lead: Former Hokkaido Bank HQ—wine and bread-bowl cheese fondue.

beer-garden.title: Sapporo Beer Garden
beer-garden.lead: Fresh draft and Hokkaido soul food—food meets industrial heritage.

garaku.title: Soup Curry GARAKU
garaku.lead: Spice warmth after arctic skiing—highly shareable soul food.

ame-yuki.title: Rain & Snow Days Café
ame-yuki.lead: Rewards on rainy/snowy days—a down-day activity when lifts close.

hero.title:
After snow,
hot-spring culture.
hero.tagline: Snow-view rotenburo to 24h sento and private baths—strategically placed

nearbyOnsen.notice: Fees, hours, tattoo rules, and late-night surcharges—check official sites.
nearbyOnsen.introLead: Beyond bathing—culture, recovery, and transit hub.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: From Honoka at Teine to Hoheikyo snow views and Otaru's 24h O-Spa—four compared picks.

nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Ordered for post-ski recovery and corridor transit.

hoheikyo.title: Hoheikyo Onsen
hoheikyo.lead: Massive outdoor bath plus authentic tandoori curry—unexpected culture mix.

teine-honoka.title: Teine Onsen Honoka
teine-honoka.lead: Full super-sento experience—hub on the descent from the hill.

o-spa.tag: 24h · 1 min from Otaru IC · salt spring
o-spa.title: Otaru Onsen O-Spa
o-spa.lead: After nightlife or before early flights—a strategic recovery stop.

hero.title:
The stay itself
is the destination.
hero.tagline: From luxury wellness to heritage hotels—five bases

corridorStay.notice: Room types, rates, and shuttles—check each property's booking site.
corridorStay.introLead: Jozankei forest retreats and Otaru heritage—each translates Hokkaido differently.
corridorStay.mapLead: Combine with ski, dining, and onsen layers.
corridorStay.spotsLead: Private onsen, gastronomy ryokan, heritage, urban explorer hotel—by need.

chalet-ivy.title: Chalet Ivy Jozankei
chalet-ivy.lead: Niseko-grade international hospitality—peak private onsen experience.

kuriyasuizan.title: Kuriyasuizan
kuriyasuizan.lead: Japanese cuisine mastery plus walkable sister properties.

omo5-otaru.title: OMO5 Otaru by Hoshino Resorts
omo5-otaru.lead: 3 min to canal—urban hotel for deep local exploration.
